theme_notes: NBA Finals tip off tomorrow (Knicks vs. Wembanyama's Spurs, first Knicks Finals since 1999); Anthropic files for IPO; Dua Lipa married Callum Turner in a Schiaparelli suit; PSG's Budapest penalty win still reverberating across Arsenal's therapy bills.
settled: PSG just won back-to-back Champions League titles. You two are also undefeated. Cherish it.
Dua Lipa got married. You two got to zero. Both are miracles.
Anthropic went public. Your balance went private. The math works out.
youOwe: Less than one Knicks nosebleed seat. Still carries the energy of a playoff foul.
Wembanyama swats more per quarter than you've paid back this month.
Petty debt, petty vibes. PSG won the whole tournament and you can't clear fifty bucks.
Dua Lipa spent this on a single wedding canapé. You're out here still owing it.
PSG won Budapest on penalties. You're still losing at 'I'll pay you back.'
NBA Finals tickets to watch Wembanyama dunk on everything — including your credit.
That's round-trip flights to Budapest. You chose to owe instead. Interesting life choices.
The EU is deporting freeloaders. Your partner has the paperwork ready.
Wembanyama's wingspan fits inside this debt. Both are genuinely concerning.
The Knicks were last in the Finals in 1999. Your payment record is equally vintage.
Anthropic just IPO'd. Your financial trajectory went the other direction.
Arsenal lost on penalties. You're about to lose something too.
Dua Lipa's Schiaparelli wedding suit cost less. There's no excuse for this.
The EU built a whole detention center for less administrative burden than you.
Wembanyama makes this per half. You've been sitting on it since spring.
More than Arsenal spent on Budapest hotel rooms they'll regret forever.
Anthropic's IPO valuation makes sense. This number does not.
At this level, your partner isn't your partner. They're a Series A investor with no equity.
theyOwe: They owe you forty bucks. Like Arsenal, they'll say it was close.
Small enough to ignore. Dangerous enough to remember. Ask PSG how penalties sneak up.
Barely registers. Like Callum Turner before Dua Lipa's wedding announcement.
They owe you one Champions League semi-final jersey. Collect like PSG collected trophies.
Wembanyama earns this in the time it took you to open this app.
That's Dua Lipa's wedding cocktail budget — owed by someone who ate your leftovers.
That's a Knicks Finals ticket. You earned it. They forgot.
The EU deports people faster than your partner repays debts. Consider the comparison.
PSG penalty kicks ran about this each. Track your own kicks accordingly.
Last time the Knicks hit the Finals was '99. Last time they paid you — also unclear.
Anthropic went public this week. Your patience has also been publicly traded to its limit.
Arsenal's players were embarrassed in Budapest. Your partner should feel something similar.
Your patience has outlasted Arsenal's Champions League campaign, and costs more.
The EU would fast-track deportation for this level of fiscal irresponsibility.
Wembanyama blocks everything. You should start blocking texts until this clears.
More than PSG's penalty bonus pool — owed to you, by a person with a Netflix account on your plan.
Anthropic's lawyers would have a word about assets this size. So should yours.
This isn't a debt. This is an IPO. File the paperwork.
